dimension_notes.naming_quality: Consistent naming conventions. NumPy-style docstrings on all public methods. Class names follow clear hierarchy: BaseModel > Miner > ImplicitRecommender. Minor deduction for 'col_names' vs 'column_names' inconsistency and some terse internal variable names.
dimension_notes.logic_clarity: Logic is generally clear with good separation. FP-Growth dispatch code in _core.py is well-structured. Minor: nested type detection in _compat.py uses string comparison rather than isinstance.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Strong typing with TYPE_CHECKING imports and Literal types for format params. Some methods still accept bare Any where more specific types would be better. from_transactions uses Any for data param.
dimension_notes.contract_coherence: Pre/post conditions documented. _check_fitted validates state. Minor: _result attribute implicitly created, some methods lack parameter validation.
dimension_notes.error_consistency: Error messages are clear and actionable. Uses specific exceptions (TypeError, ValueError, RuntimeError). Some paths silently catch Exception with pass.
dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: Good use of mixins (RuleMinerMixin, EmbeddingMixin). _type_utils centralizes type checks. Type detection in _compat.py still uses __name__ string comparison instead of isinstance.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: Recently improved: extracted is_dataframe_empty helper, consolidated duplicate import_optional_dependency calls, replaced inline lambdas with to_list_if_collection. Remaining: model.py still 1100+ lines with 5 classes that could potentially be split.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: Clear BaseModel > Miner / ImplicitRecommender / SequentialRecommender hierarchy. model.py houses all base classes which makes it a large file but keeps the hierarchy visible. Clean delegation to export.py, viz.py, similarity.py.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: Well-structured method-level design. from_transactions pattern consistent across all classes. Minor: _prepare_transactions and _fit_transactions in ImplicitRecommender could be streamlined.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Improved: removed redundant imports, duplicate computations, consolidated lambdas. Remaining: verbose timing pattern repeated in _core.py, try/except for optional polars import repeated.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: Good module boundaries: _core.py for Rust dispatch, _compat.py for coercions, _dependencies.py for optional imports, _type_utils.py for type helpers. Lazy imports prevent circular dependencies.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: Factory pattern (from_transactions) cleanly separates construction from fitting. EmbeddingMixin decouples vector DB integration. Minor: some __init__ methods have many parameters.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: Consistent use of: verbose:int not bool, seed:int not random_state, NumPy docstrings. Minor outlier: import_optional_dependency still imported inside try blocks in some places rather than at function top.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: Clean optional dependency management via import_optional_dependency. No pinned versions in pyproject.toml for optional deps. Strong separation of core (numpy/scipy) from optional (pandas/polars/spark).
dimension_notes.test_strategy: Comprehensive test suite (767+ tests). Good use of Hypothesis for property testing. Benchmark tests separate from unit tests. Minor: some optional dependency modules lack direct test coverage.
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: Consistent API across miners (mine/predict/association_rules) and recommenders (fit/recommend_items/recommend_users). Literal types added for format params. from_pandas/from_polars/from_spark aliases consistent.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: Not applicable — this is a computational library with no authentication or authorization.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: fit_transactions deprecated with proper DeprecationWarning. recommend_items on Miner properly documented as deprecated alias. Some old patterns (bare string format params) partially migrated.
dimension_notes.package_organization: Flat package structure appropriate for library size. _core.py, _compat.py, _dependencies.py, _type_utils.py provide clean internal utilities. Public modules (als.py, bpr.py, etc.) each contain one primary class.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: Unified design philosophy: Rust for compute, Python for API surface. Consistent pattern of validate-in-Python, process-in-Rust. Zero-copy data handling philosophy clear across Pandas/Polars/Arrow paths.

issues.summary: model.py at 1100+ lines contains 5 classes. Consider splitting into model/_base.py, model/_miner.py, model/_recommender.py
evidence: model.py contains RuleMinerMixin (160 lines), BaseModel (270 lines), Miner (350 lines), ImplicitRecommender (300 lines), SequentialRecommender (30 lines) — all in one file
issues.suggestion: Split into a model/ package with __init__.py re-exporting all classes for backward compatibility

issues.summary: _compat.py detects DataFrame types via __name__ string comparison instead of isinstance
evidence: Lines 29 and 37: type(data).__name__ == 'Table' and type(data).__name__ == 'DataFrame' used instead of isinstance checks
issues.suggestion: Use try/except imports with isinstance for cleaner type detection

issues.summary: Pattern of try: import polars except ImportError: is_polars = False repeated in model.py and _core.py
evidence: model.py lines 851-858 and _core.py contain similar try/except blocks for optional polars detection
issues.suggestion: Create a helper like has_polars() in _dependencies.py or _type_utils.py

issues.summary: Miner.recommend_items is deprecated in favor of recommend_for_cart but still present without DeprecationWarning
evidence: recommend_items on Miner delegates to recommend_for_cart but may not emit DeprecationWarning consistently

issues.summary: from_transactions and several methods accept data: Any instead of using the DataFrame type alias
evidence: from_transactions(data: Any, ...) could use DataFrame = Union[pd.DataFrame, pl.DataFrame, ...] from _compat.py
issues.suggestion: Use the existing DataFrame type alias from _compat.py for TYPE_CHECKING annotations

issues.summary: _result attribute implicitly created during predict/mine but not initialized in __init__
evidence: BaseModel and Miner rely on _result being set by predict() but it is not declared in __init__
issues.suggestion: Initialize self._result = None in BaseModel.__init__ or use __init_subclass__

issues.summary: import_optional_dependency imported inside try blocks rather than at function scope in some files
evidence: In _prepare_transactions, the import is inside a try block. Other functions import it at function top.
issues.suggestion: Move import to function top, wrap only the polars check in try/except

issues.summary: Optional modules like viz.py, optuna.py, grouped.py lack direct test coverage
evidence: These modules are only tested transitively through integration tests, not with dedicated unit tests
issues.suggestion: Add lightweight smoke tests that import and exercise basic API of each optional module

issues.summary: Some internal variables use abbreviated names like u_col, i_col, _pd, _pl that reduce readability
evidence: model.py: u_col, i_col, u_data, i_data. _core.py: col_names abbreviated context. transactions.py: _pd, _pl for pandas/polars modules
issues.suggestion: Use descriptive names: user_col, item_col, user_data for public-facing code; short names acceptable in tight loops

issues.summary: _convert_to_orig_type in Miner has deeply nested if/elif branches for type conversion
evidence: Miner._convert_to_orig_type checks _orig_df_type against 'pyarrow', 'polars', 'spark', 'pandas' in a long if/elif chain
issues.suggestion: Refactor to a dispatch dict mapping type strings to converter functions

issues.summary: Some exception handlers silently swallow errors without logging or re-raising
evidence: _compat.py line 55: except Exception as e followed by warnings.warn but some paths in model.py catch broadly
issues.suggestion: Add logging.debug for caught exceptions to aid debugging, especially in framework coercion code

issues.summary: model.py mixes mixin, ABC base, and concrete base patterns in one file making the hierarchy harder to navigate
evidence: RuleMinerMixin, BaseModel(ABC), Miner(BaseModel), ImplicitRecommender(BaseModel, EmbeddingMixin) all in one 1100-line file
issues.suggestion: Group related classes into sub-modules: mixins.py, base.py, miner.py, recommender.py

issues.summary: ImplicitRecommender has both _prepare_transactions and _fit_transactions which have overlapping responsibilities
evidence: _fit_transactions calls _prepare_transactions then .fit(). This two-step pattern is confusing compared to from_transactions
issues.suggestion: Deprecate _fit_transactions, make from_transactions + explicit .fit() the only path

issues.summary: Lazy imports in several modules prevent circular imports but make dependency flow less clear
evidence: import_optional_dependency called inside methods rather than at module level; _compat import inside functions
issues.suggestion: Document the import dependency graph; consider using __init__.py lazy loading pattern more systematically

issues.summary: ALS.__init__ accepts 15+ parameters which creates tight coupling to constructor
evidence: ALS.__init__ takes factors, regularization, alpha, iterations, seed, verbose, cg_iters, use_cholesky, use_eals, eals_iters, alpha_view, view_target, use_biases, popularity_weighting
issues.suggestion: Group related params into dataclasses: SolverConfig, EalsConfig, BiasConfig

issues.summary: recommend_items means different things on Miner (deprecated cart recommender) vs ImplicitRecommender (user recommendations)
evidence: Miner.recommend_items is a deprecated alias for recommend_for_cart. ImplicitRecommender.recommend_items is the primary recommendation method.
issues.suggestion: Fully deprecate Miner.recommend_items and ensure documentation clearly distinguishes the two semantics

issues.summary: All base model classes in a single model.py file rather than a model/ package
evidence: model.py contains 5 classes totaling 1100+ lines. Each concrete model (als.py, bpr.py) is separate but all bases share one file
issues.suggestion: Create rusket/model/ package with __init__.py re-exporting for backward compatibility

issues.summary: Some methods return Pandas DataFrames by default while others default to Polars
evidence: export_factors defaults to format='pandas', batch_recommend defaults to format='polars'. Inconsistent default return types.
issues.suggestion: Standardize on one default format or make it configurable at model construction time

issues.summary: Optional dependencies in pyproject.toml lack upper version bounds which could lead to breaking changes
evidence: Optional deps like pandas, polars, pyarrow are listed without version constraints (e.g. 'pandas' instead of 'pandas>=1.4,<3')
issues.suggestion: Add minimum and maximum version bounds for optional dependencies based on tested compatibility
